title: Ensure that forward let-binding references have been implemented correctly
analysis:
The cold-start evaluation path in Engine::eval() (lib.rs lines 679-722) evaluates Let bindings by iterating template.value_cells in source (declaration) order. When a let binding references another let declared later in the same scope, the referenced let hasn't been evaluated yet and its value is missing from the ValueMap. eval_expr calls values.get_or_undef(), which returns Value::Undef for missing references, producing incorrect results.

The same bug exists in the post-resolution re-evaluation loop (lib.rs lines 837-878), where let bindings are re-evaluated after auto param resolution, again in source order. If a forward-referencing let depends on another let that depends on a resolved auto param, the source-order re-evaluation produces stale/incorrect values.

The incremental path (edit_param) does NOT have this bug because it uses compute_eval_set() which calls topological_sort(), ensuring dependencies are evaluated before dependents regardless of declaration order.

The fix is to topologically sort let bindings before evaluating them in both cold-start locations. The topological_sort utility in dirty.rs and the extract_dependency_trace utility in deps.rs already exist and can be reused.

No existing tests exercise forward let-binding references — all existing tests declare let bindings in dependency order (e.g., x before y where y=x+1).

steps.description: Write failing test forward_let_ref_cold_start_simple in incremental.rs: template with param a (default 5, Int), let y = x + 1 (forward ref to x, declared first), let x = a + 10 (declared second). Assert cold-start produces x=15, y=16. Currently fails because y evaluates before x and gets Undef via get_or_undef.

Fix cold-start let evaluation in Engine::eval() to topologically sort let bindings before evaluating. Replace the source-order 'for cell in &template.value_cells' loop (lines 679-722) with: (1) collect all Let cells with expressions into a HashSet<NodeId>, (2) build HashMap<NodeId, DependencyTrace> via extract_dependency_trace for each, (3) call topological_sort from dirty.rs, (4) iterate sorted result, looking up each cell from value_cells by ID to evaluate. This reuses existing infrastructure and makes step-1 pass.

Write test forward_let_ref_cold_start_deep_reverse_chain in incremental.rs: all let bindings in fully reversed dependency order. Template: param a (default 0, Int), let z = y + 1 (declared 1st), let y = x + 1 (declared 2nd), let x = a + 1 (declared 3rd). Assert x=1, y=2, z=3. Exercises 3-deep topological sort in cold-start. Should pass after step-2 fix.

Write test forward_let_ref_cold_start_diamond in incremental.rs: diamond dependency with forward refs. Template: param a (default 10, Int), let d = b + c (forward refs to both, declared 1st), let b = a + 1 (declared 2nd), let c = a + 2 (declared 3rd). Assert b=11, c=12, d=23. Exercises converging forward references. Should pass after step-2 fix.

Write failing test forward_let_ref_post_resolution in incremental.rs: forward ref during post-resolution re-evaluation. Template: param p (default mm(1.0), length), auto x (length), let c = b + x (forward ref to b, declared before b), let b = x + p (declared after c). MockConstraintSolver resolves x=mm(10.0). After cold-start: x=0.01 SI, b=x+p=0.01+0.001=0.011 SI, c=b+x=0.011+0.01=0.021 SI. Test asserts c has correct value. Fails because post-resolution re-eval iterates in source order, evaluating c before b is re-evaluated with resolved x.

Fix post-resolution let re-evaluation in Engine::eval() (lines 837-878) to topologically sort let bindings before re-evaluating. Apply the same pattern as step-2: collect Let cells into NodeId set, build trace map, topological_sort, then evaluate in sorted order. This makes step-5 pass.

Write test forward_let_ref_incremental_edit_param in incremental.rs: verify incremental path handles forward refs correctly. Template: param a (default 5, Int), let y = x + 1 (forward ref), let x = a + 10. Cold-start → x=15, y=16. Edit a from 5 to 20 → x=30, y=31. Confirms incremental topo sort (already present in compute_eval_set) handles forward-declared lets correctly after param edit.

Write test forward_let_ref_cold_start_matches_incremental in incremental.rs: verify cold-start and incremental produce identical values for a forward-ref template. Template: param a (default 3, Int), let c = b * 2 (forward ref), let b = a + 7. Cold-start: b=10, c=20. Edit a to 13 → b=20, c=40. Then create a fresh Engine with a=13 as default and cold-start eval. Assert fresh cold-start values match incremental edit values. Proves both evaluation paths produce consistent results for forward-ref templates.

design_decisions.decision: Reuse existing topological_sort from dirty.rs rather than implementing a new sort
design_decisions.rationale: topological_sort already handles NodeId sets with DependencyTrace maps, handles duplicate reads (a*a), and has comprehensive unit tests. Using it for cold-start let evaluation requires only building the NodeId/trace inputs, not new sorting infrastructure.

design_decisions.decision: Sort only Let cells during topological ordering, not all value_cells
design_decisions.rationale: Params are roots (no dependencies) and are already evaluated in pass 1 before any let evaluation. Auto params start as Undef. Only Let-to-Let forward references need reordering. The topological sort will naturally put lets with no let-dependencies first.

design_decisions.decision: Apply topological sort to both cold-start evaluation and post-resolution re-evaluation
design_decisions.rationale: Both code paths iterate value_cells in source order and are vulnerable to the same forward-reference bug. The post-resolution path re-evaluates let bindings after auto params are resolved — if a let references another let that depends on an auto param, source-order iteration uses the pre-resolution (Undef/stale) value instead of the freshly resolved one.

design_decisions.decision: Place all tests in the existing incremental.rs test file
design_decisions.rationale: The tests exercise Engine::eval() and Engine::edit_param() behavior, which is the same domain as existing incremental.rs tests. The test patterns (CompiledModuleBuilder, TopologyTemplateBuilder, expression builders) are already imported there. For 6 additional tests, co-location is cleaner than a separate file.

reuse.how: Call with HashSet<NodeId> of Let cells and HashMap<NodeId, DependencyTrace> built from their expressions. Returns Vec<NodeId> in dependency-first order. Used in cold-start eval to replace source-order iteration.
Call for each Let cell's default_expr to get its DependencyTrace (list of ValueCellId reads). Feed into topological_sort's trace map.
Fluent builders for test fixtures. Use .let_binding() calls in specific orders to exercise forward references — the order of calls determines source order in value_cells.
Build CompiledExpr trees for let binding expressions and constraint expressions in tests.
MockConstraintChecker for tests without constraints. MockConstraintSolver for the post-resolution forward-ref test (step-5) to provide resolved auto param values.
